lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਦੇਵੈ ਕਾਹੂ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak devai kaahoo |4|2|
lines.english: Says Nanak, rare are those who are blessed with this Boat. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਪ ਪਥਰ ਤਰਣੁ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: paap pathar taran na jaaee |
lines.english: Sin is a stone which does not float.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਉ ਬੇੜਾ ਜੀਉ ਚੜਾਊ ॥
lines.translit: bhau berraa jeeo charraaoo |
lines.english: So let the Fear of God be the boat to carry your soul across.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਘਰੁ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 ghar 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, First Mehl, First House:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਣੀ ਕਾਗਦੁ ਮਨੁ ਮਸਵਾਣੀ ਬੁਰਾ ਭਲਾ ਦੁਇ ਲੇਖ ਪਏ ॥
lines.translit: karanee kaagad man masavaanee buraa bhalaa due lekh pe |
lines.english: Actions are the paper, and the mind is the ink; good and bad are both recorded upon it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਜਿਉ ਕਿਰਤੁ ਚਲਾਏ ਤਿਉ ਚਲੀਐ ਤਉ ਗੁਣ ਨਾਹੀ ਅੰਤੁ ਹਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jiau jiau kirat chalaae tiau chaleeai tau gun naahee ant hare |1|
lines.english: As their past actions drive them, so are mortals driven. There is no end to Your Glorious Virtues, Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਬਿਸਰਤ ਤੇਰੇ ਗੁਣ ਗਲਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har bisarat tere gun galiaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Forgetting the Lord, your own virtues shall rot away. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਿਤ ਚੇਤਸਿ ਕੀ ਨਹੀ ਬਾਵਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: chit chetas kee nahee baavariaa |
lines.english: Why do you not keep Him in your consciousness, you mad man?

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਸਿ ਰਸਿ ਚੋਗ ਚੁਗਹਿ ਨਿਤ ਫਾਸਹਿ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਮੂੜੇ ਕਵਨ ਗੁਣੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: ras ras chog chugeh nit faaseh chhoottas moorre kavan gunee |2|
lines.english: With relish and delight, you continually bite at the bait; you are trapped, you fool - how will you ever escape? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਲੀ ਰੈਨਿ ਜਾਲੁ ਦਿਨੁ ਹੂਆ ਜੇਤੀ ਘੜੀ ਫਾਹੀ ਤੇਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: jaalee rain jaal din hooaa jetee gharree faahee tetee |
lines.english: The night is a net, and the day is a net; there are as many traps as there are moments.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇਆ ਆਰਣੁ ਮਨੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਲੋਹਾ ਪੰਚ ਅਗਨਿ ਤਿਤੁ ਲਾਗਿ ਰਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: kaaeaa aaran man vich lohaa panch agan tith laag rahee |
lines.english: The body is a furnace, and the mind is the iron within it; the five fires are heating it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਇਆ ਮਨੂਰੁ ਕੰਚਨੁ ਫਿਰਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਜੇ ਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਤਿਨੇਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: bheaa manoor kanchan fir hovai je gur milai tinehaa |
lines.english: What was turned to slag is again transformed into gold, if one meets with the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਇਲੇ ਪਾਪ ਪੜੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਊਪਰਿ ਮਨੁ ਜਲਿਆ ਸੰਨੑੀ ਚਿੰਤ ਭਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: koeile paap parre tis aoopar man jaliaa sanaee chint bhee |3|
lines.english: Sin is the charcoal placed upon it, which burns the mind; the tongs are anxiety and worry. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਓਹੁ ਦੇਵੈ ਤਉ ਨਾਨਕ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਟਸਿ ਦੇਹਾ ॥੪॥੩॥
lines.translit: ek naam amrit ohu devai tau naanak trisattas dehaa |4|3|
lines.english: He blesses the mortal with the Ambrosial Name of the One Lord, and then, O Nanak, the body is held steady. ||4||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਮਲ ਮਝਾਰਿ ਬਸਸਿ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਜਲ ਪਦਮਨਿ ਜਾਵਲ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: bimal majhaar basas niramal jal padaman jaaval re |
lines.english: In the pure, immaculate waters, both the lotus and the slimy scum are found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਦਮਨਿ ਜਾਵਲ ਜਲ ਰਸ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੰਗਿ ਦੋਖ ਨਹੀ ਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: padaman jaaval jal ras sangat sang dokh nahee re |1|
lines.english: The lotus flower is with the scum and the water, but it remains untouched by any pollution. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਦਰ ਤੂ ਕਬਹਿ ਨ ਜਾਨਸਿ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: daadar too kabeh na jaanas re |
lines.english: You frog, you will never understand.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਖਸਿ ਸਿਬਾਲੁ ਬਸਸਿ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਜਲ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਨ ਲਖਸਿ ਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhakhas sibaal basas niramal jal amrit na lakhas re |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You eat the dirt, while you dwell in the immaculate waters. You know nothing of the ambrosial nectar there. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਸੁ ਜਲ ਨਿਤ ਨ ਵਸਤ ਅਲੀਅਲ ਮੇਰ ਚਚਾ ਗੁਨ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: bas jal nit na vasat aleeal mer chachaa gun re |
lines.english: You dwell continually in the water; the bumble bee does not dwell there, but it is intoxicated with its fragrance from afar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੰਦ ਕੁਮੁਦਨੀ ਦੂਰਹੁ ਨਿਵਸਸਿ ਅਨਭਉ ਕਾਰਨਿ ਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: chand kumudanee doorahu nivasas anbhau kaaran re |2|
lines.english: Intuitively sensing the moon in the distance, the lotus bows its head. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਖੰਡੁ ਦੂਧਿ ਮਧੁ ਸੰਚਸਿ ਤੂ ਬਨ ਚਾਤੁਰ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: amrit khandd doodh madh sanchas too ban chaatur re |
lines.english: The realms of nectar are irrigated with milk and honey; you think you are clever to live in the water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨਾ ਆਪੁ ਤੂ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਛੋਡਸਿ ਪਿਸਨ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਜਿਉ ਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: apanaa aap too kabahu na chhoddas pisan preet jiau re |3|
lines.english: You can never escape your own inner tendencies, like the love of the flea for blood. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਸੰਗਿ ਵਸਹਿ ਜਨ ਮੂਰਖ ਆਗਮ ਸਾਸ ਸੁਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: panddit sang vaseh jan moorakh aagam saas sune |
lines.english: The fool may live with the Pandit, the religious scholar, and listen to the Vedas and the Shaastras.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਪਾਖੰਡੀ ਨਾਮਿ ਨ ਰਾਚਹਿ ਇਕਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਚਰਣੀ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: eik paakhanddee naam na raacheh ik har har charanee re |
lines.english: Some are hypocrites; they do not merge with the Naam, the Name of the Lord. Some are absorbed in the Feet of the Lord, Har, Har.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨਾ ਆਪੁ ਤੂ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਛੋਡਸਿ ਸੁਆਨ ਪੂਛਿ ਜਿਉ ਰੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: apanaa aap too kabahu na chhoddas suaan poochh jiau re |4|
lines.english: You can never escape your own inner tendencies, like the crooked tail of the dog. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਬਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਪਾਵਸਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਰਸਨਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਿ ਰੇ ॥੫॥੪॥
lines.translit: poorab likhiaa paavas naanak rasanaa naam jap re |5|4|
lines.english: The mortals obtain what they are predestined to receive; O Nanak, with your tongue, chant the Naam. ||5||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, First Mehl,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ॥
lines.translit: salok |
lines.english: Salok:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਠਸਠਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਨਾਮੁ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਸੁ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਭਾਗ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: atthasatth teerath naam prabh naanak jis masatak bhaag |1|
lines.english: The merits of the sixty-eight places of pilgrimage are found in God's Name, O Nanak, when such destiny is inscribed upon one's forehead. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਤਿਤ ਪੁਨੀਤ ਅਸੰਖ ਹੋਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਚਰਨੀ ਮਨੁ ਲਾਗ ॥
lines.translit: patit puneet asankh hohi har charanee man laag |
lines.english: Countless sinners are sanctified, attaching their minds to the Feet of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਣਿ ਸਹ ਕੀ ਇਕ ਬਾਤ ਸੁਹੇਲੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sun seh kee ik baat suhelee |1|
lines.english: listen to this one joyous story of your Husband Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: sabad |
lines.english: Shabad:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਖੀ ਸਹੇਲੀ ਗਰਬਿ ਗਹੇਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: sakhee sahelee garab gahelee |
lines.english: O friends and companions, so puffed up with pride,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਦੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਖਰੀ ਰੰਞਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: hau dohaagan kharee ranyaanee |
lines.english: I am a dejected, discarded bride, totally miserable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਮੈ ਬੇਦਨ ਸਾ ਕਿਸੁ ਆਖਾ ਮਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jo mai bedan saa kis aakhaa maaee |
lines.english: Who can I tell about my pain, O my mother?

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਜੀਉ ਨ ਰਹੈ ਕੈਸੇ ਰਾਖਾ ਮਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har bin jeeo na rahai kaise raakhaa maaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Without the Lord, my soul cannot survive; how can I comfort it, O my mother? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਿਜਮਤਿ ਕਰੀ ਜਨੁ ਬੰਦਾ ਤੇਰਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: khijamat karee jan bandaa teraa |3|
lines.english: I serve You as Your humble slave. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਦਾਨਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸਿਰਿ ਮੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: too daanaa saahib sir meraa |
lines.english: You are my wise Lord and Master, above my head.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਇਆ ਸੁ ਜੋਬਨੁ ਧਨ ਪਛੁਤਾਣੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: geaa su joban dhan pachhutaanee |2|
lines.english: I have lost my youth; I regret and repent. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਦਰਸਨ ਕੈਸੇ ਰਵਉ ਸਨੇਹੀ ॥੪॥੫॥
lines.translit: bin darasan kaise rvau sanehee |4|5|
lines.english: without the Blessed Vision of my Beloved, how can I enjoy Him? ||4||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਣਤਿ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਅੰਦੇਸਾ ਏਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: bhanat naanak andesaa ehee |
lines.english: Nanak humbly prays, this is my only concern:
